flavour_text: The backs of a few filled the stomachs of many. The harvest begins modestly enough, yet somehow the baskets keep returning heavier than expected and the storehouses fuller than anyone planned for. By evening, entire streets smell of fresh bread and simmering broth. For the first time in many years, people can eat without the guilt of who will go without.
season_i: Each time this round a Farm is activated for its Resource production, gain 1 additional Food.
season_ii: The first two times this round Farms are activated for Resource production, gain 1 additional Food and 1 additional Goods.
season_iii: Each time this round a Farm is activated for its Resource production, gain 2 additional resources; choose Food or Goods each time.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: One thousand swings of the pickaxe opens up a new path
flavour_text: The miners speak of stubborn stone finally giving way after weeks of careful labor. Buried tunnels reconnect to forgotten depths. Word spreads quickly, the mountain has decided to yield a little more of itself.
season_i: Each time this round a Mine is activated for its Resource production, gain 1 additional Stone.
season_ii: The first two times this round Mines are activated for Resource production, gain 1 additional Stone and 1 additional Metal.
season_iii: Each time this round a Mine is activated for its Resource production, gain 2 additional resources; choose Stone or Metal each time.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Forests are carefully thinned instead of recklessly destroyed. The foresters move with patience rarely seen in the old age, sparing saplings and harvesting fallen trunks before touching living giants. In return, hidden game trails, overgrown pathways, and long-forgotten groves slowly reveal themselves once more.
season_i: Each time this round a Forest is activated for its Resource production, gain 1 additional Wood.
season_ii: The first two times this round Forests are activated for Resource production, gain 2 additional Wood.
season_iii: Each time this round a Forest is activated for its Resource production, gain 2 additional resources; choose Wood or Food each time.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: The rains that we sheltered from now yield the bounty of nature.
flavour_text: Even the rot provides enrichment for the land and ensures the tiny things thrive. What once felt like ruin now nourishes renewal as heavy rains soften the earth, moss spreads thick across fallen stone, herbs flourish and life quietly returns.
season_i: Each time this round a Wildlands is activated for its Resource production, gain 1 additional Herbs.
season_ii: The first two times this round Wildlands are activated for Resource production, gain 2 additional Herbs.
season_iii: Each time this round a Wildlands is activated for its Resource production, gain 2 additional resources; choose Herbs or Food each time.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Once tightened belts and hoarded pelts now burst into celebration. For a few precious nights, caution loosens its grip upon the settlement. Lanterns line the roads as spare goods are traded freely.
season_i: The next Merchant Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 1 fewer resource of your choice.
season_ii: The next Merchant Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 2 fewer Resources total.
season_iii: One Merchant Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 0 Resources; it still costs its normal action.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Even deep within the collapsed vaults, time and the elements spared some precious items. From within tightly bound hide, star charts, mathematical formulas and agricultural knowledge reveal themselves. The advanced level of comprehension this ancient civilization possessed has finally emerged from the ashes.
season_i: The next time this round a Dig Site is activated for Resource production, gain 1 additional Metal.
season_ii: The next time this round a Dig Site is activated for Resource production, gain 1 additional Metal and 1 additional Goods.
season_iii: Each time this round a Dig Site is activated for its Resource production, gain 2 additional resources; choose Metal or Goods each time.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Hands and hearts remember what was once lost as the square fills with timber carvings, woven cloth, leatherwork, and tools polished with quiet pride. Old trades once abandoned during the wars return beside crowded stalls and cookfires, and for the first time in many years, craftsmanship feels like culture again rather than necessity alone.
season_i: The next Crafting Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 1 fewer resource of your choice.
season_ii: The next Crafting Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 2 fewer Resources total.
season_iii: One Crafting Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 0 Resources.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: What is written in the stars can finally be heeded
flavour_text: For much of the old age, no one had the luxury of studying the heavens beyond prayers for survival. Now, with peaceful nights, entire possibilities emerge simply because people finally have time enough to look upward.
season_i: Look at the top 3 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in the same order.
season_ii: Look at the top 4 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in any order.
season_iii: Look at the top 6 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in any order.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: A new pair of hands begins quietly taking on more responsibility. They ask questions eagerly, reading every letter and ledger that had been piling up.
season_i: The next Resource Tile placed this round costs 0 Resources
season_ii: The next Resource or Housing Tile placed this round costs 0 Resources
season_iii: The next tile placed this round costs 0 Resources
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: Feuds soften as warm hearths, green heaths and safe havens are found
flavour_text: Old rivalries rarely vanish outright. Instead they fade slowly beneath shared meals and working alongside one another. Folk who once met with suspicion now pause to exchange greetings across garden walls and along common paths.
season_i: The next Housing Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 1 fewer resource of your choice.
season_ii: The next Housing Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 2 fewer Resources total.
season_iii: One Housing Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 0 Resources.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: When the roads filled once more
flavour_text: At first it is only the occasional traveler. Then merchants, wandering families, messengers, and supply wagons begin appearing with increasing regularity. The roads become understood for what they mean as much as what they are.
season_i: The next Travel Tile placed this round costs 0 Actions.
season_ii: The next Travel Tile placed or upgraded this round costs 0 Actions.
season_iii: The next two Travel Tiles placed or upgraded this round cost 0 Actions.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Even in difficult seasons, the settlement has learned the value of sharing what it can spare. Extra goods find their way into struggling homes as gifts, repairs, and small comforts that ease the weight of hardship. A community willing to provide for one another can soften strains that no single family could bear alone.
season_i: You may spend 3 Goods to remove 1 Strain
season_ii: You may spend 4 Goods to remove up to 2 Strain from one tile
season_iii: You may spend 5 Goods to remove up to 3 Strain split across up to 2 tiles
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.

card_name: The scent of herb and tonic
flavour_text: Herbal smoke drifts across the settlement as tonics, poultices, and old remedies are prepared deep into the night. The scent alone brings comfort to weary households, while shared draughts and tinctures help the community recover from sickness and exhaustion.
season_i: You may spend 4 Herbs to remove 1 Strain
season_ii: You may spend 5 Herbs to remove up to 2 Strain from one tile
season_iii: You may spend 6 Herbs to remove up to 3 Strain split across up to 2 tiles
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: A light on the long dark. Lanterns illuminated the way to a safer day
flavour_text: New lantern posts now line the settlement’s roads and crossings, their warm glow cutting through fog, ruin, and uncertainty. Reinforced with fresh metal and careful craftsmanship, these lights guide workers and travelers safely onward, restoring confidence to paths once left abandoned in the dark.
season_i: You may spend 4 Metal to remove 1 Strain
season_ii: You may spend 5 Metal to remove up to 2 Strain from one Travel or Housing tile
season_iii: You may spend 6 Metal to remove up to 3 Strain split across up to 2 Travel or Housing tiles
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: The shape of the land reveals hints to its past. As soft earth is scraped away, ancient footings emerge to support the future of the realm.
season_i: The next Housing Tile placed this round costs 2 fewer Wood or Stone.
season_ii: The next Housing Tile placed this round costs 4 fewer Wood or Stone.
season_iii: The next Housing Tile placed this round costs 0 Resources.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Resolve the current Season effect, then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Stones are carried, beams are raised, meals are shared, and work that once seemed too heavy for a few becomes possible through many willing hands.
season_i: The next tile placed this round costs 1 fewer resource of your choice.
season_ii: The next two tiles placed this round each cost 1 fewer resource of your choice.
season_iii: The next two tiles placed or upgraded this round each cost 2 fewer resources total.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

card_name: Clear nights make for clear plans
flavour_text: As the night grows calm, the clouds part and the settlement grows quiet. Lanterns burn late over maps and ledgers. The future remains uncertain, but for now at least, a path as clear as the night sky is mapped.
season_i: Look at the top 2 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in any order.
season_ii: Look at the top 3 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in any order.
season_iii: Look at the top 4 cards of the Encounter Deck, then return them in any order, then you may place the bottom one on top.

flavour_text: Gates left open a moment longer can change who makes it through
season_i: Add 1 timer token to 1 active Arrival, up to the normal maximum of 3 timer tokens.
season_ii: Add up to 2 timer tokens divided among active Arrivals, up to the normal maximum of 3 timer tokens on each Arrival.
season_iii: Add up to 3 timer tokens divided among active Arrivals, up to the normal maximum of 3 timer tokens on each Arrival.

flavour_text: A burden carried together does not weigh the same.
season_i: Keep this card face-up. The next time players resolve an active Burden, reduce its resource cost by 2 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
season_ii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players resolve an active Burden, reduce its resource cost by 4 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
season_iii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players resolve an active Burden, reduce its resource cost by 6 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: What was tended before the storm is easier to mend after it.
season_i: Remove 1 Strain from 1 Supported tile.
season_ii: Remove 1 Strain from up to 2 Supported tiles.
season_iii: Remove 1 Strain from up to 3 Supported tiles.

flavour_text: A steady hand can make a hard place whole again.
season_i: Choose each tile occupied by one or more Steward Tokens. For each chosen tile, remove 1 Strain from it. For each chosen tile that had no Strain, gain 1 resource of your choice instead. Gain no more than 2 total resources this way.
season_ii: Choose each tile occupied by one or more Steward Tokens. For each chosen tile, remove 1 Strain from it. For each chosen tile that had no Strain, gain 2 resources of your choice instead. Gain no more than 4 total resources this way.
season_iii: Choose each tile occupied by one or more Steward Tokens. For each chosen tile, remove 1 Strain from it. For each chosen tile that had no Strain, gain 3 resources of your choice instead. Gain no more than 6 total resources this way.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: What was needed had not been lost, only set aside.
season_i: Exchange up to 2 resources in the Warehouse for the same number of resources of any type.
season_ii: Exchange up to 4 resources in the Warehouse for the same number of resources of any type.
season_iii: Exchange up to 6 resources in the Warehouse for the same number of resources of any type.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: Some work rises quickly when weather, will, and hands align.

season_ii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players upgrade a Core Tile, reduce that upgrade's resource cost by 2 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
season_iii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players upgrade a Core Tile, reduce that upgrade's resource cost by 3 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: When welcome becomes work, the Realm lends hands.
season_i: Keep this card face-up. The next time players complete an Arrival, reduce its resource Requirement by 1 resource of your choice. Then discard this card.
season_ii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players complete an Arrival, reduce its resource Requirement by 2 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
season_iii: Keep this card face-up. The next time players complete an Arrival, reduce its resource Requirement by 3 resources of your choice. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: The Realm does not always mend gently. Sometimes it simply refuses to break.
season_i: Remove up to 2 Strain from 1 Overstrained tile. If no Strain was removed this way, remove 1 Strain from 1 placed tile instead.
season_ii: Remove up to 2 Strain from 1 Overstrained tile. If no Strain was removed this way, remove 1 Strain from up to 2 placed tiles instead.
season_iii: Remove up to 2 Strain from up to 2 Overstrained tiles. If no Strain was removed this way, remove 1 Strain from up to 3 placed tiles instead.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three stacked Season rows with left I/II/III markers. Move repeated lifecycle text to footer.

flavour_text: The farmland was pushed beyond its limits during the war years. Crops were stripped from exhausted soil until only blight remained. The people say the land can heal, but only through ritual, patience, and care. Until then, every failed harvest reminds the settlement that even the earth remembers hardship.
season_i: Choose 1 Farm with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Farm with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Farm with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent placed tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay Herbs based on the current Season: Season I 2 Herbs; Season II 4 Herbs; Season III 6 Herbs. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: Entire groves vanished to feed the engines of war. Spears, barricades, and pyres consumed ancient trees faster than the forest could recover. Now the woodland answers with twisted paths and restless spirits lingering between the trunks. Respect must be offered before the forest yields its bounty again.
season_i: Choose 1 Forest with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Forest with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Forest with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent placed tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay resources of your choice based on the current Season: Season I 2 resources; Season II 4 resources; Season III 6 resources. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: Something wounded during the conflict fled beneath the mines and never returned to the surface. Miners speak of distant scraping and heavy breathing echoing through flooded tunnels. Whatever hides below no longer understands peace, only intrusion. Until it is calmed, the depths remain dangerous.
season_i: Choose 1 Mine with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Mine with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Mine with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent Travel Tile or Resource Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay Food based on the current Season: Season I 2 Food; Season II 4 Food; Season III 6 Food. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: When the armies retreated, they abandoned beasts too broken or too unruly. Now they roam the wildlands unchecked, trampling fences and scattering workers. Give them boundaries, and they may yet settle.
season_i: Choose 1 Wildlands with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Wildlands with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Wildlands with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent Housing Tile or Travel Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.

flavour_text: Centuries of damp rot have fused manuscript pages together into a delicate solid block. Forcing them open would surely destroy them. The right herbs and processes could unbind the pages, and with careful drying the fragile parchment can be saved.
season_i: Choose 1 Dig Site with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Dig Site with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Dig Site with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent placed tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.

flavour_text: The workshops burn day and night, filling the settlement with smoke, sparks, and the endless clang of labor. At first the people welcomed the sound of rebuilding, but exhaustion settles heavily when homes stand too close to the furnaces. Tempers grow shorter with every passing season.
season_i: Choose 1 Housing Tile adjacent to a Crafting Tile, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Housing Tiles adjacent to Crafting Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Housing Tiles adjacent to Crafting Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The merchants still trade what remains from the old war stores: dented shields, cracked armor, and relics of conflict nobody truly needs anymore. Meanwhile, the settlement asks for shovels, tools, and practical goods to rebuild daily life. Frustration grows as markets fill with the past instead of the future.
season_i: Choose 1 Housing Tile adjacent to a Merchant Tile, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Housing Tiles adjacent to Merchant Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Housing Tiles adjacent to Merchant Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The gathering halls remain full, but the air within them is stagnant. Crowded rooms, shared cups, and long evenings together carry sickness quietly through the settlement. What began as a lingering cough now spreads from home to home wherever the community gathers for warmth and comfort.
season_i: Choose 1 Housing Tile adjacent to a Social Tile, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Housing Tiles adjacent to Social Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Housing Tiles adjacent to Social Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The settlement’s markets grow faster than its workshops can sustain. Merchants continue taking orders while crafters are struggling to keep up. Resentment builds as orders remain unfulfilled.
season_i: Choose 1 Merchant Tile or Crafting Tile adjacent to the other category, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 Merchant Tile and 1 Crafting Tile, each adjacent to the other category and with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 2 Merchant Tiles and 2 Crafting Tiles, each adjacent to the other category and with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

flavour_text: The settlement rushed to carve new roads through land still marked by the old war. Workers uncover trenches, ruined fortifications, and forgotten dangers buried beneath the soil. Each new route becomes harder to maintain as the past resurfaces beneath the foundations of progress.
season_i: Choose 1 Travel Tile adjacent to a Resource Tile, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Travel Tiles adjacent to Resource Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Travel Tiles adjacent to Resource Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

flavour_text: The settlement depends on confidence as much as stone or timber. When homes grow strained and fewer lanterns burn at dusk, hesitation spreads outward into the roads themselves. Routes once filled with movement slowly choke beneath fear, rumor, and uncertainty.
season_i: Choose 1 Travel Tile adjacent to a Housing Tile with 1 or more Strain, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Travel Tiles adjacent to Housing Tiles with 1 or more Strain, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Travel Tiles adjacent to Housing Tiles with 1 or more Strain, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The houses protect the people from wind and rain, but little else. Bare timber walls, sparse furnishings, and cold interiors leave the settlement feeling temporary, as though everyone still expects another war to arrive tomorrow. The people long for signs of comfort, beauty, and permanence before despair settles too deeply into daily life.
season_i: Choose 1 Housing Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Housing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 3 Housing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: These social spaces provide warmth, music, and shared meals. Now their stores are running thin, their tables grow emptier, and every missed delivery deepens the strain on those tasked with keeping spirits alive. A settlement can survive hunger for a time, but community fades quickly when generosity becomes impossible.
season_i: Choose 1 Social Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Social Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 3 Social Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Goods or place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The roads stretch faster than the settlement can care for them. Paths once laid with optimism now sit lonely at the edge of lantern light, where rain gathers in ruts and travellers hesitate before continuing. A road without nearby hands to maintain it soon becomes another kind of wilderness.
season_i: Choose 1 Travel Tile not adjacent to a Housing Tile, with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Travel Tiles not adjacent to Housing Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Travel Tiles not adjacent to Housing Tiles, each with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay Wood based on the current Season: Season I 2 Wood; Season II 4 Wood; Season III 6 Wood. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

card_name: Too Many Houses, Too Little Homes
flavour_text: New homes rise quickly, but walls alone do not make people feel settled. Cold rooms, thin meals, and missing comforts remind the people how fragile safety can feel when growth outruns care. The settlement must prove these houses are more than temporary shelter.
season_i: Choose 1 Housing Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 1 Food or 1 Goods, or place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Housing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Food or 1 Goods, or place 1 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 3 Housing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. For each chosen tile, pay 1 Food or 1 Goods, or place 1 Strain on it.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay resources based on the current Season: Season I 2 Food or Goods in any combination; Season II 4 Food or Goods in any combination; Season III 6 Food or Goods in any combination. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: Ledgers that once seemed clear no longer agree. Barrels are marked twice, crates are missing seals, and no one can say whether boxes of perishables are new or old stock.
season_i: Choose Wood, Stone, or Food. Lose 2 of that resource, or choose 1 Resource Tile with fewer than 3 Strain and place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose Wood, Stone, Metal, Food, or Herbs. Lose 3 of that resource, or choose 1 Resource Tile with fewer than 3 Strain and place 1 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose any non-Goods resource. Lose 5 of that resource, or choose 2 Resource Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain and place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: Word travels faster than preparation. The settlement’s welcome has become known across the roads, and with that reputation comes expectation. Those who arrive do not ask for luxury, but lacking the basics is a poor comparison to what they have dreamed of.
season_i: Choose 1 active Arrival. Pay 1 Goods or remove 1 timer token from it. If there are no active Arrivals, this Burden has no effect.
season_ii: Choose 2 active Arrivals. For each chosen Arrival, pay 1 Goods or remove 1 timer token from it. If there are no active Arrivals, this Burden has no effect.
season_iii: Choose 3 active Arrivals. For each chosen Arrival, pay 1 Goods or remove 1 timer token from it. If there are no active Arrivals, this Burden has no effect.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: Peace does not end every hurt at once. A song, a smell of smoke, a name spoken carelessly. Small things can resurface old pain. The settlement must learn that some wounds may need to be tended to for a while to come.
season_i: Choose 1 Social Tile or Wellbeing Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 2 Herbs or place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 2 Social Tiles or Wellbeing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 4 Herbs or place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Choose 3 Social Tiles or Wellbeing Tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. Pay 6 Herbs or place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
lifecycle_or_resolution:
Place this card on the Stewards Board as an active Burden.
To resolve: Spend 1 Action and pay Herbs based on the current Season: Season I 2 Herbs; Season II 4 Herbs; Season III 6 Herbs. Then discard this card.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Three larger Season blocks with left I/II/III markers. Put short To resolve text in relevant block when applicable.

flavour_text: The shelves are full in one corner and bare in another.
season_i: Identify the resource type with the most markers in the Warehouse. Lose 2 of that resource, or choose 1 placed tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Identify the resource type with the most markers in the Warehouse. Lose 4 of that resource, or choose 2 placed tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.
season_iii: Identify the resource type with the most markers in the Warehouse. Lose 6 of that resource, or choose 3 placed tiles with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile.

season_iii: Choose 1 upgraded Core Tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 2 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent placed tile with fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.

flavour_text: Kind words fade when care cannot follow.

flavour_text: Even silence can split when left untended.
season_i: Choose 1 tile with 1 or more Strain and fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_ii: Choose 1 tile with 1 or more Strain and fewer than 3 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it. Then choose 1 adjacent placed tile with 0 Strain. Place 1 Strain on it.
season_iii: Choose 1 Overstrained tile. Then choose 2 adjacent placed tiles with 0 Strain. Place 1 Strain on each chosen tile. If there are no Overstrained tiles, resolve the Season II effect instead.

flavour_text: The riders arrive slowly, cloaks heavy with rain and years alike. Their banners are gone, their rations worn thin, yet the horses remain immaculate. Brushed, fed, and spoken to with a tenderness rarely afforded in the old age. They ask not for barracks nor glory. Only pastureland, fresh water, and somewhere both rider and steed may finally rest
requirement:
2 Wood
4 Herbs
2 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Complete by spending 1 Interact action while the Requirement is fulfilled. Pl...
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: They arrive aboard a vessel stitched together with timber taken from forgotten ports and ruined warships alike. The sailors speak little of where they came from, only that safe harbors became rare things. Even before they unload their cargo, they begin surveying the riverbanks and speaking hopefully of peaceful trade and travel.
requirement:
2 Wood
4 Herbs
2 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

flavour_text: They travel with painted wagons and weathered scripts rescued from abandoned halls. In village squares and roadside clearings they perform stories of the old realm — not to glorify war, but to ensure its cost is never softened by time. While the children laugh at the interval comedies, the elders rarely speak during the performances.
requirement:
4 Wood
4 Metal
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Complete by spending 1 Interact action while the Requirement is fulfilled. Pl...
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: Their caravan carries no weapons, only tools wrapped in linen and limbs carved from polished wood and brass. Once battlefield surgeons and armorers, they now dedicate themselves to quieter repairs. In their care, no one is abandoned. They are taught how to endure differently.
requirement:
4 Wood
4 Metal
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: The scholars who arrive are young enough to have barely known the breadth of the war, yet old enough to inherit its scars. They carry fragments of maps, surviving histories, and bundles of half-preserved books. Their wish is simple: that the next generation learns how the realm was broken, and how it might avoid breaking again.
requirement:
2 Goods
2 Herbs
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Complete by spending 1 Interact action while the Requirement is fulfilled. Pl...
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: The shelter is raised before the travelers even ask permission to stay. Canvas awnings become walls, wagons become kitchens, and by nightfall lantern light already spills from its open doors. The folk who run it make no distinction between orphan, laborer, refugee, or wanderer. Any soul without warmth, food, or a place to sleep is welcomed inside without question. In the Quiet Vale, such places are treasured more than castles.
requirement:
2 Goods
2 Herbs
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Complete by spending 1 Interact action while the Requirement is fulfilled. Pl...
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: He arrives laughing loudly enough to silence an entire tavern. Great casks follow behind him, strapped to wagons reinforced more heavily than most fortifications. He insists that a settlement survives not merely on roofs and food, but on evenings where songs drown out memory, if only for a little while.
requirement:
8 Wood
4 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Complete by spending 1 Interact action while the Requirement is fulfilled. Pl...
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: The traveler sleeps beside glass apparatus that bubbles softly through the night. Villagers whisper that they are dangerous. Though probably correct,  where supplies run short, they somehow find ways to make the impossible merely expensive instead

card_name: Lay down the tools of destruction
flavour_text: The old armor is surrendered piece by piece. Helms become lantern casings. Swords are repurposed into  ploughs. The veterans overseeing the work speak little. The damage on many of these items already say too much.

2 Metal

flavour_text: The caretakers of the shrine claim the land remembers kindness. They bury offerings beneath the first seeds of every season and leave portions of each harvest untouched for wandering spirits and hungry birds alike. Whether by blessing or patience, their fields rarely fail.

flavour_text: Their armor mismatches, functional but clearly oft repaired, and their reports suspiciously modest. When burdens grow too dangerous for common folk, these wanderers shoulder them without ceremony.

4 Goods

flavour_text: The messengers arrive exhausted yet restless, as though standing still itself has become unnatural. They map roads instinctively, measure distance by habit, and remember every settlement they pass through. In a fractured realm, they believe knowledge may be the first true bridge rebuilt.

5 Food
5 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: Speaking softly, their ancient words seep into roots older than any kingdom. Where they walk, the wildlands grow calmer, the inhabitants less fearful. Some villagers leave offerings at the forest edge now. Others simply listen when the wind moves through the branches.
requirement:
6 Herbs

flavour_text: The miners faces are painted with dust, sweat and scrapes by rock edge, yet they still speak of the deep earth with reverence rather than greed. They claim every mountain has its moods, and disasters begin the moment folk stop listening to the stones.
requirement:
6 Food

flavour_text: They plant saplings knowing they will never sit beneath their shade. This is their duty. The old realm consumed forests faster than memory can recall. This new one, they insist, will be enduring.
requirement:
6 Herbs

2 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

card_name: What came before the last age
flavour_text: They come carrying fragments older than any living memory: carved lintels, weathered tablets, and stories preserved in the pauses between words. None can say who first built beneath these hills, only that the settlement now stands among echoes it has not yet learned to understand.
requirement:
6 Stone
6 Metal
2 Goods

flavour_text: The Beastmaster's reputation arrives long before he does. Yet the feared commander who finally enters the settlement spends his evenings tending lame oxen and brushing exhausted draft horses. He speaks more gently to animals than his resting snarl would let you believe.
requirement:
2 Wood

flavour_text: The barbarian carries an axe large enough to split shield and body in a single stroke, though these days it fells only deadwood. She claims forests regrow more honestly than kingdoms do. The children adore her despite the scars, and even with a single arm, the timber camps have never felt safer.
requirement:
2 Wood
2 Stone
6 Food
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

card_name: From Songs of War to the Search for Ore
flavour_text: The dwarves sing while they work, though the songs are quieter now than in generations past. Their old marching hymns have become work chants for picks and cart crews. Even so, deep underground, the reverberation of those dulcet tones sooths the soul of the mountain.
requirement:
2 Wood
2 Stone
6 Food
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

card_name: From Blade Swingers to Herb Stringers
flavour_text: They arrive carrying bundles of drying herbs hung carefully from staves polished smooth through years of travel. Whether preparing tinctures or trimming medicinal roots, there is an unmistakable discipline to their hands, the sort learned through repetition long before they ever considered such peaceful work.

card_name: From Dark Decay to Light Display
flavour_text: They come not to plunder these long quiet ruins, but to resurface the truths of the ancient past. So preocupied with war, the last age cast aside such ideas of learning from somthing that had fallen. Now those rescued secrets may serve as a foundation for a realm trying to rebuild.
requirement:
2 Wood
2 Stone
6 Food
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: They arrive as a wagonload. People of strong backs, calloused hands, and a need to be useful. They ask for modest meals, safe shelter, and work that builds rather than breaks. By dusk, foundations are being cleared twice as fast as before.
requirement:
4 Food
4 Stone
4 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: Their carts are hung with polished lamps and spare lenses wrapped carefully in cloth. These travellers have crossed too many unsafe roads to take the darkness lightly. They offer to mark the settlement’s routes with steady flame, so that those who must travel late may still find their way home.
requirement:
4 Wood
4 Metal
2 Goods
within three rounds. Place three tokens on this card to track.

layout_note: Tarot portrait. Type bar + title band. Full flavour text on parchment slip. Rules panel has Requirement and Reward only; do not add separate Timer field.

flavour_text: They come carrying seeds, recipies and small iron cooking pots blackened by years of use. Wherever they settle, gardens appear beside doorways and shared fires are tended long into the evening. They do not promise miracles, only that no one should face hardship alone.

flavour_text: Deep beneath collapsed stone and tangled roots, workers uncover the bell tower of an older age. Though dulled by centuries beneath the earth, the bell still carries a strange warmth beneath the hand. When finally raised and rung across the valley, its sound travels impossibly far. Days later, travelers begin arriving along roads long thought abandoned.
effect: When revealed, choose 3 Arrival Cards from the game box. Shuffle those chosen cards, reveal 1 at random, and place it on the Stewards Board as an active Arrival with 3 timer tokens. Return the other chosen Arrival Cards to the box. Then discard this card.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Golden Boons are distinct Encounter Cards. They do not use Season fields and may break normal rules only as specified by their Effect text.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Same back as Encounter deck. More ornate front within shared family. Use one bespoke Effect block; no Season rows.

flavour_text: The traveler appears only after dusk, wrapped in pale cloth that never seems touched by rain or mud. They ask for little beyond a place beside the fire. Yet as the settlement sleeps, a quiet melody drifts through the night air. Mournful, ancient, and impossibly calming. Come morning, the exhaustion of the season feels strangely lighter.
effect: When revealed, after the normal Player Turns phase this round, the group immediately resolves one additional Player Turns phase before Phase Four. Do not seed or reveal Encounter Cards, remove Arrival timers, resolve other end-of-round effects, or advance the Round Timer for this additional phase. Each player takes one additional turn with the normal action allowance for the current player count. Then discard this card.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Golden Boons are distinct Encounter Cards. They do not use Season fields and may break normal rules only as specified by their Effect text.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Same back as Encounter deck. More ornate front within shared family. Use one bespoke Effect block; no Season rows.

flavour_text: The ring is discovered within a collapsed cairn beneath the mines, its gold untouched by time. Though no living soul recalls the ruler whose seal it bears, old instincts awaken around it. Roads are rerouted. Foundations shifted as though the settlement briefly remembers how kingdoms were once ordered
effect: When revealed, choose up to 5 placed tiles. Remove those chosen tiles, then place each chosen tile into a legal empty map space. Chosen tiles may be placed into spaces vacated by other chosen tiles. Ignore adjacency and reachability restrictions for these placements, but terrain restrictions still apply. If moving a multi-hex tile, every covered hex in the new position must be empty and legal. Chosen tiles keep Strain, Supported, upgrade state, and any tokens. Recalculate Travel Networks and Overstrained effects immediately. Then discard this card.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Golden Boons are distinct Encounter Cards. They do not use Season fields and may break normal rules only as specified by their Effect text.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Same back as Encounter deck. More ornate front within shared family. Use one bespoke Effect block; no Season rows.

flavour_text: The scroll arrives sealed in gold thread without messenger or explanation. Its contents shift subtly between readings. Maps becoming letters, warnings becoming opportunities, forgotten names resurfacing in fresh ink. Some swear the parchment records possibilities rather than truths, offering glimpses of futures not yet chosen.
effect: When revealed, each player may discard any number of standard Encounter Cards from their own hand. For each card discarded this way, that player draws 1 random standard Encounter Card from the game box into their hand. Golden Boons may not be drawn this way. If there are not enough standard Encounter Cards in the box, draw as many as possible. Then discard this card.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Golden Boons are distinct Encounter Cards. They do not use Season fields and may break normal rules only as specified by their Effect text.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Same back as Encounter deck. More ornate front within shared family. Use one bespoke Effect block; no Season rows.

flavour_text: The vial is uncovered within the hollow roots of a pale, dead tree deep in the wildlands. Clear glass, carefully sealed in golden wax that has somehow never cracked. Inside swirls a thick golden liquid that catches the light like captured dawn. Those entrusted to carry it find their journeys strangely favored. storms soften, paths reveal themselves, and even the wilderness seems reluctant to bar their way.
effect: When revealed, this effect remains for the rest of the game: once per round, during any player's turn, that player may take the Travel to a Disconnected Tile action without spending an Action. All normal destination and placement rules still apply. Then discard this card.
lifecycle_or_resolution: Golden Boons are distinct Encounter Cards. They do not use Season fields and may break normal rules only as specified by their Effect text.
layout_note: Tarot portrait. Same back as Encounter deck. More ornate front within shared family. Use one bespoke Effect block; no Season rows.
